<observed_from_primary_session>

  <requested_at>2026-06-14</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision | security_alert | security_note ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

--- MODE SWITCH: PROGRESS SUMMARY ---
⚠️ CRITICAL TAG REQUIREMENT — READ CAREFULLY:
• You MUST wrap your ENTIRE response in <summary>...</summary> tags.
• Do NOT use <observation> tags. <observation> output will be DISCARDED and cause a system error.
• The ONLY accepted root tag is <summary>. Any other root tag is a protocol violation.

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
# 获取 Android system_server coredump 的方法汇总

[知识来源: Web 搜索 - "Android system_server coredump 抓取", "tombstoned intercept", "/data/vendor/core system_server RLIM_INFINITY", "core_dump Android init.rc core_pattern"]
[知识来源: AOSP master - android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/debuggerd.cpp]
[知识来源: AOSP master - android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/tombstoned/tombstoned.cpp]
[知识来源: AOSP mirror - github.com/aosp-mirror/platform_system_core/blob/main/debuggerd/client/debuggerd_client.cpp]
[知识来源: AOSP master - android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md]
[置信度: 高]

需要先澄清一个关键事实：**Android 平台官方默认提供的"system_server crash dump"是 tombstone（墓碑），而不是 ELF coredump**。这两种产物在功能、位置、抓取方式上都不同，下面按从易到难的顺序讲清楚。

---

## 1️⃣ 必知前提：tombstone ≠ coredump

| 维度 | Tombstone | ELF coredump |
|---|---|---|
| **是否可加载到 GDB** | ❌ 不能（自定义文本/proto 格式） | ✅ 直接 `gdb binary core`，可看任意变量、寄存器、内存 |
| **包含内容** | 所有线程栈、寄存器、内存映射、fd 列表 | **进程完整地址空间快照**（含 heap/stack/.so） |
| **典型大小** | 几百 KB ~ 几 MB | 几十 MB ~ 几 GB（取决于 RSS） |
| **默认路径** | `/data/tombstones/tombstone_XX`（由 tombstoned 守护进程写入） | `/data/core/`、`/cores/` 或 `/data/vendor/core/`（由 kernel 通过 `core_pattern` 启动用户态 handler 写入） |
| **Android 默认是否启用** | ✅ 是（system_server 等所有 native 进程崩溃都会自动生成） | ❌ 默认关闭（需要 userdebug/eng build + setprop） |
| **AOSP 关键代码** | `debuggerd/client/debuggerd_client.cpp` + `debuggerd/tombstoned/tombstoned.cpp` | `init.rc` 中 `write /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern \| ...` |

---

## 2️⃣ 最常用：先拿 tombstone（任何 build 都能用）

### 2.1 system_server 崩溃后自动抓
system_server 是 zygote fork 出来的，它的 native crash 走标准链路：

```
native 崩溃
  → linker 安装的 signal handler (SIGSEGV/SIGBUS/SIGABRT/...)
  → debuggerd client (libdebuggerd_client) 把请求发给 tombstoned
  → tombstoned 生成 /data/tombstones/tombstone_NN
```

抓取：
```bash
adb shell ls -la /data/tombstones/        # 列出最近 32 个（属性 tombstoned.max_tombstone_count 可调）
adb pull /data/tombstones/tombstone_05 ./   # 拉本地
```

### 2.2 system_server 还在跑（不死但想看栈）
AOSP `debuggerd/debuggerd.cpp`（master 分支）入口签名：
```cpp
// debuggerd.cpp: usage
usage: debuggerd [-bj] PID
  -b, --backtrace    只取栈，不写完整 tombstone
  -j                 取 Java 线程栈
```
```bash
# 必须 root
adb root
adb shell ps -A | grep system_server     # 拿到 PID，例如 1234
adb shell debuggerd -b 1234              # 直接打 stdout
adb shell debuggerd 1234                 # 完整 tombstone 写到 /data/tombstones
adb shell debuggerd -j 1234              # 抓 Java 栈（ApplicationExitInfo 也行）
```

源码验证（`debuggerd.cpp`）：
```cpp
// 调用 debuggerd_trigger_dump(proc_info.pid, dump_type, 0, std::move(output_fd))
// 通过 socket 连接 tombstoned -> crash_dump -> ptrace 目标进程 -> 生成 tombstone
```

**注意**：debuggerd 只对 `getuid() == 0` 开放，所以必须先 `adb root`。

---

## 3️⃣ 进阶：抓 ELF coredump（推荐 userdebug/eng 设备）

如果要进 GDB 看 Java 对象内存、GC 状态、ART 字段，**只有 coredump 才能做**。

### 3.1 system_server 的特殊麻烦
system_server 是 zygote fork 出来的，zygote **不会**给 fork 出来的子进程设 `RLIMIT_CORE = RLIM_INFINITY`。这是 Android 故意为之：避免系统服务崩溃时 dump 巨大 core 把设备写满。

社区做法（参考 init.rc + CSDN/Android 源码分析）：
```bash
# 1) 启用 persist.debug.trace，init.rc 会：
#    - mkdir /data/core 0777
#    - write /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern "/data/core/%E.%p.%e"
#    - 给后续 fork 的进程 setrlimit RLIM_INFINITY
adb shell setprop persist.debug.trace 1
adb reboot

# 2) 触发 system_server native crash 后：
adb shell ls -la /data/core/
# 产物形如：system_server.1234.system_server
```

**AOSP 源码路径**：上面这条机制来自 `frameworks/base/core/jni/com_android_internal_os_Zygote.cpp` 的 `ForkAndSpecializeCommon()`：
```cpp
// Zygote fork 子进程后，如果 persist.debug.trace=1
// -> postForkChild -> nativePostForkChild 中执行 setrlimit(RLIMIT_CORE, RLIM_INFINITY, ...)
```

### 3.2 Android 14+ 更现代的做法：core_pattern 接 socket
AOSP 的 `init/README.md` 提到 `socket` 指令语法，新版本 platform 已经支持：
```rc
# init.rc (userdebug build)
service coredump /system/bin/coredump
    socket coredump seqpacket+passcred
```

也可以直接走 `init` 内置能力：把 `core_pattern` 改成 `|/system/bin/coredump_handler` 这种"pipe to userspace handler"形式（kernel 通过 `fork+exec` 调用，并把 core 内容从 stdin 喂进去），handler 把 core 重定向到 `/cores/` 或 `/data/vendor/core/`。

**注意**：Android 14+ 上 `setrlimit` + `core_pattern` 这条老链路在 SELinux 收紧后**经常被 deny**。如果不行，用下面 3.3 的方法绕过。

### 3.3 手动注入：attach 后 `gcore`
不依赖任何配置，直接用 gdb：
```bash
adb root
adb forward tcp:5039 tcp:5039

# 本机：
gdb-multiarch
(gdb) set sysroot /path/to/android/symbols/
(gdb) file out/target/product/xxx/symbols/system/bin/app_process
(gdb) target remote :5039
(gdb) attach <system_server_pid>
(gdb) gcore /tmp/system_server.core      # ← 直接生成 ELF core
(gdb) detach
(gdb) quit

adb pull /tmp/system_server.core ./
```
**优点**：不受 SELinux、user/userdebug、core_pattern 限制
**缺点**：会 STW 短暂挂起进程，且 `gcore` 与 ART GC 兼容性需要在 ART 暂停点执行（生产环境慎用）

---

## 4️⃣ 三种产物的取舍

| 你的目标 | 推荐产物 | 抓法 |
|---|---|---|
| 看 system_server 崩溃线程栈 | tombstone | 默认就有，`adb pull /data/tombstones/` |
| 看 system_server 所有线程在某个瞬间的状态 | `debuggerd -b` | `adb shell debuggerd -b <pid>` |
| 进 GDB 看 Java 对象、native heap、ART 镜像对象 | ELF coredump | `setprop persist.debug.trace 1` 重启 + 触发崩溃，或 `gdb attach` 后 `gcore` |
| 看 Java ANR 主线程栈 | traces.txt | `/data/anr/anr_XX`，或 `debuggerd -j` |

---

## 5️⃣ 实战建议（针对你的 array klass 损坏问题）

由于你前面在做 ART `klass` 损坏诊断，**强烈建议抓 ELF coredump**，原因：
- tombstone 里只看得见栈和寄存器，**看不到 Java 对象内存内容**
- 你需要的是 `print *(uint32_t*)obj` 这种 GDB 命令直接看对象头
- 还需要 `print obj->klass_.reference_` 这种 mirror::Object 字段访问

抓 core 后用：
```bash
gdb out/target/product/xxx/symbols/system/bin/app_process
(gdb) set solib-search-path symbols/system/lib64:symbols/system/lib
(gdb) core system_server.core
(gdb) thread apply all bt           # 所有线程栈
(gdb) print obj_addr->klass_       # 直接看 klass 字段
(gdb) x/16wx obj_addr              # 看对象前 64 字节
```
这样就能把"klass 值异常" vs "对象所在卡片边界" vs "TLAB 边界"这些维度一次性查清楚。

---

## 📚 知识来源（Step 4 输出格式要求）

**Web 搜索（4 引擎并行）**
- Google: `q="Android system_server coredump native crash tombstones debug.ptrace options"`
- DDG: `q="Android get core dump system_server process tombstone debug coredump pattern"`
- Tavily (advanced): `q="Android 14 coredump handler userspace init.rc core_pattern socket substitute"`
- MiniMax（中文）: `q="Android system_server coredump 抓取 native crash 系统服务 调试"`
- 补充: `q="debuggerd_trigger_dump AOSP client.cpp signal"`、`q="tombstoned intercept system_server client_manager backtrace signal AOSP"`

**AOSP 源码交叉验证（必须）**
- https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/debuggerd.cpp — 确认 `debuggerd [-bj] PID` 接口、`debuggerd_trigger_dump` 调用链
- https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/tombstoned/tombstoned.cpp — 确认 `/data/tombstones/` 路径、`max_concurrent_dumps_ = 1`、`tombstoned.max_tombstone_count` 属性可调
- https://github.com/aosp-mirror/platform_system_core/blob/main/debuggerd/client/debuggerd_client.cpp — 确认 `BIONIC_SIGNAL_DEBUGGER` + `sigqueue(tid, signal, val)` 是触发机制
- https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md — 确认 `socket` 指令语法（Android 14+ core_dump socket 化路径）

**置信度标注**
- `debuggerd` 命令行接口、tombstone 路径、trigger_dump 链路 → **高**（4 引擎 + AOSP 源码全部命中）
- `setprop persist.debug.trace 1` 触发 RLIM_INFINITY 的 init.rc 细节 → **中**（中文社区文档命中，AOSP 源码路径在不同版本上名字略不同，需结合 `com_android_internal_os_Zygote.cpp` 的具体 fork 流程确认）
- Android 14+ 的 `core_pattern | /system/bin/handler` socket 化路径 → **中**（AOSP init 文档确认支持 socket 指令，但具体 core_dump 守护进程是否默认启用依设备厂商定制）
- gcore 绕道方案 → **高**（gdb 标准命令，与 Android 版本无关）

---

如果你已经定位到具体的 system_server 崩溃触发路径（比如知道是在某个 system service 的哪个 native 函数），告诉我后我可以给你更精确的"最小化抓 core"工作流，避免一次抓出几 GB 的 core。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

REMINDER: Your response MUST use <summary> as the root tag, NOT <observation>.
IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Claude's Full Response to User:
# 获取 Android system_server coredump 的方法汇总

[知识来源: Web 搜索 - "Android system_server coredump 抓取", "tombstoned intercept", "/data/vendor/core system_server RLIM_INFINITY", "core_dump Android init.rc core_pattern"]
[知识来源: AOSP master - android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/debuggerd.cpp]
[知识来源: AOSP master - android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/tombstoned/tombstoned.cpp]
[知识来源: AOSP mirror - github.com/aosp-mirror/platform_system_core/blob/main/debuggerd/client/debuggerd_client.cpp]
[知识来源: AOSP master - android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md]
[置信度: 高]

需要先澄清一个关键事实：**Android 平台官方默认提供的"system_server crash dump"是 tombstone（墓碑），而不是 ELF coredump**。这两种产物在功能、位置、抓取方式上都不同，下面按从易到难的顺序讲清楚。

---

## 1️⃣ 必知前提：tombstone ≠ coredump

| 维度 | Tombstone | ELF coredump |
|---|---|---|
| **是否可加载到 GDB** | ❌ 不能（自定义文本/proto 格式） | ✅ 直接 `gdb binary core`，可看任意变量、寄存器、内存 |
| **包含内容** | 所有线程栈、寄存器、内存映射、fd 列表 | **进程完整地址空间快照**（含 heap/stack/.so） |
| **典型大小** | 几百 KB ~ 几 MB | 几十 MB ~ 几 GB（取决于 RSS） |
| **默认路径** | `/data/tombstones/tombstone_XX`（由 tombstoned 守护进程写入） | `/data/core/`、`/cores/` 或 `/data/vendor/core/`（由 kernel 通过 `core_pattern` 启动用户态 handler 写入） |
| **Android 默认是否启用** | ✅ 是（system_server 等所有 native 进程崩溃都会自动生成） | ❌ 默认关闭（需要 userdebug/eng build + setprop） |
| **AOSP 关键代码** | `debuggerd/client/debuggerd_client.cpp` + `debuggerd/tombstoned/tombstoned.cpp` | `init.rc` 中 `write /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern \| ...` |

---

## 2️⃣ 最常用：先拿 tombstone（任何 build 都能用）

### 2.1 system_server 崩溃后自动抓
system_server 是 zygote fork 出来的，它的 native crash 走标准链路：

```
native 崩溃
  → linker 安装的 signal handler (SIGSEGV/SIGBUS/SIGABRT/...)
  → debuggerd client (libdebuggerd_client) 把请求发给 tombstoned
  → tombstoned 生成 /data/tombstones/tombstone_NN
```

抓取：
```bash
adb shell ls -la /data/tombstones/        # 列出最近 32 个（属性 tombstoned.max_tombstone_count 可调）
adb pull /data/tombstones/tombstone_05 ./   # 拉本地
```

### 2.2 system_server 还在跑（不死但想看栈）
AOSP `debuggerd/debuggerd.cpp`（master 分支）入口签名：
```cpp
// debuggerd.cpp: usage
usage: debuggerd [-bj] PID
  -b, --backtrace    只取栈，不写完整 tombstone
  -j                 取 Java 线程栈
```
```bash
# 必须 root
adb root
adb shell ps -A | grep system_server     # 拿到 PID，例如 1234
adb shell debuggerd -b 1234              # 直接打 stdout
adb shell debuggerd 1234                 # 完整 tombstone 写到 /data/tombstones
adb shell debuggerd -j 1234              # 抓 Java 栈（ApplicationExitInfo 也行）
```

源码验证（`debuggerd.cpp`）：
```cpp
// 调用 debuggerd_trigger_dump(proc_info.pid, dump_type, 0, std::move(output_fd))
// 通过 socket 连接 tombstoned -> crash_dump -> ptrace 目标进程 -> 生成 tombstone
```

**注意**：debuggerd 只对 `getuid() == 0` 开放，所以必须先 `adb root`。

---

## 3️⃣ 进阶：抓 ELF coredump（推荐 userdebug/eng 设备）

如果要进 GDB 看 Java 对象内存、GC 状态、ART 字段，**只有 coredump 才能做**。

### 3.1 system_server 的特殊麻烦
system_server 是 zygote fork 出来的，zygote **不会**给 fork 出来的子进程设 `RLIMIT_CORE = RLIM_INFINITY`。这是 Android 故意为之：避免系统服务崩溃时 dump 巨大 core 把设备写满。

社区做法（参考 init.rc + CSDN/Android 源码分析）：
```bash
# 1) 启用 persist.debug.trace，init.rc 会：
#    - mkdir /data/core 0777
#    - write /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern "/data/core/%E.%p.%e"
#    - 给后续 fork 的进程 setrlimit RLIM_INFINITY
adb shell setprop persist.debug.trace 1
adb reboot

# 2) 触发 system_server native crash 后：
adb shell ls -la /data/core/
# 产物形如：system_server.1234.system_server
```

**AOSP 源码路径**：上面这条机制来自 `frameworks/base/core/jni/com_android_internal_os_Zygote.cpp` 的 `ForkAndSpecializeCommon()`：
```cpp
// Zygote fork 子进程后，如果 persist.debug.trace=1
// -> postForkChild -> nativePostForkChild 中执行 setrlimit(RLIMIT_CORE, RLIM_INFINITY, ...)
```

### 3.2 Android 14+ 更现代的做法：core_pattern 接 socket
AOSP 的 `init/README.md` 提到 `socket` 指令语法，新版本 platform 已经支持：
```rc
# init.rc (userdebug build)
service coredump /system/bin/coredump
    socket coredump seqpacket+passcred
```

也可以直接走 `init` 内置能力：把 `core_pattern` 改成 `|/system/bin/coredump_handler` 这种"pipe to userspace handler"形式（kernel 通过 `fork+exec` 调用，并把 core 内容从 stdin 喂进去），handler 把 core 重定向到 `/cores/` 或 `/data/vendor/core/`。

**注意**：Android 14+ 上 `setrlimit` + `core_pattern` 这条老链路在 SELinux 收紧后**经常被 deny**。如果不行，用下面 3.3 的方法绕过。

### 3.3 手动注入：attach 后 `gcore`
不依赖任何配置，直接用 gdb：
```bash
adb root
adb forward tcp:5039 tcp:5039

# 本机：
gdb-multiarch
(gdb) set sysroot /path/to/android/symbols/
(gdb) file out/target/product/xxx/symbols/system/bin/app_process
(gdb) target remote :5039
(gdb) attach <system_server_pid>
(gdb) gcore /tmp/system_server.core      # ← 直接生成 ELF core
(gdb) detach
(gdb) quit

adb pull /tmp/system_server.core ./
```
**优点**：不受 SELinux、user/userdebug、core_pattern 限制
**缺点**：会 STW 短暂挂起进程，且 `gcore` 与 ART GC 兼容性需要在 ART 暂停点执行（生产环境慎用）

---

## 4️⃣ 三种产物的取舍

| 你的目标 | 推荐产物 | 抓法 |
|---|---|---|
| 看 system_server 崩溃线程栈 | tombstone | 默认就有，`adb pull /data/tombstones/` |
| 看 system_server 所有线程在某个瞬间的状态 | `debuggerd -b` | `adb shell debuggerd -b <pid>` |
| 进 GDB 看 Java 对象、native heap、ART 镜像对象 | ELF coredump | `setprop persist.debug.trace 1` 重启 + 触发崩溃，或 `gdb attach` 后 `gcore` |
| 看 Java ANR 主线程栈 | traces.txt | `/data/anr/anr_XX`，或 `debuggerd -j` |

---

## 5️⃣ 实战建议（针对你的 array klass 损坏问题）

由于你前面在做 ART `klass` 损坏诊断，**强烈建议抓 ELF coredump**，原因：
- tombstone 里只看得见栈和寄存器，**看不到 Java 对象内存内容**
- 你需要的是 `print *(uint32_t*)obj` 这种 GDB 命令直接看对象头
- 还需要 `print obj->klass_.reference_` 这种 mirror::Object 字段访问

抓 core 后用：
```bash
gdb out/target/product/xxx/symbols/system/bin/app_process
(gdb) set solib-search-path symbols/system/lib64:symbols/system/lib
(gdb) core system_server.core
(gdb) thread apply all bt           # 所有线程栈
(gdb) print obj_addr->klass_       # 直接看 klass 字段
(gdb) x/16wx obj_addr              # 看对象前 64 字节
```
这样就能把"klass 值异常" vs "对象所在卡片边界" vs "TLAB 边界"这些维度一次性查清楚。

---

## 📚 知识来源（Step 4 输出格式要求）

**Web 搜索（4 引擎并行）**
- Google: `q="Android system_server coredump native crash tombstones debug.ptrace options"`
- DDG: `q="Android get core dump system_server process tombstone debug coredump pattern"`
- Tavily (advanced): `q="Android 14 coredump handler userspace init.rc core_pattern socket substitute"`
- MiniMax（中文）: `q="Android system_server coredump 抓取 native crash 系统服务 调试"`
- 补充: `q="debuggerd_trigger_dump AOSP client.cpp signal"`、`q="tombstoned intercept system_server client_manager backtrace signal AOSP"`

**AOSP 源码交叉验证（必须）**
- https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/debuggerd.cpp — 确认 `debuggerd [-bj] PID` 接口、`debuggerd_trigger_dump` 调用链
- https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/debuggerd/tombstoned/tombstoned.cpp — 确认 `/data/tombstones/` 路径、`max_concurrent_dumps_ = 1`、`tombstoned.max_tombstone_count` 属性可调
- https://github.com/aosp-mirror/platform_system_core/blob/main/debuggerd/client/debuggerd_client.cpp — 确认 `BIONIC_SIGNAL_DEBUGGER` + `sigqueue(tid, signal, val)` 是触发机制
- https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md — 确认 `socket` 指令语法（Android 14+ core_dump socket 化路径）

**置信度标注**
- `debuggerd` 命令行接口、tombstone 路径、trigger_dump 链路 → **高**（4 引擎 + AOSP 源码全部命中）
- `setprop persist.debug.trace 1` 触发 RLIM_INFINITY 的 init.rc 细节 → **中**（中文社区文档命中，AOSP 源码路径在不同版本上名字略不同，需结合 `com_android_internal_os_Zygote.cpp` 的具体 fork 流程确认）
- Android 14+ 的 `core_pattern | /system/bin/handler` socket 化路径 → **中**（AOSP init 文档确认支持 socket 指令，但具体 core_dump 守护进程是否默认启用依设备厂商定制）
- gcore 绕道方案 → **高**（gdb 标准命令，与 Android 版本无关）

---

如果你已经定位到具体的 system_server 崩溃触发路径（比如知道是在某个 system service 的哪个 native 函数），告诉我后我可以给你更精确的"最小化抓 core"工作流，避免一次抓出几 GB 的 core。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

REMINDER: Your response MUST use <summary> as the root tag, NOT <observation>.
IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!
